data.title: Financial Services Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Financial Services. Exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires at 4:20 ET, 12:20 ET and 16:50 ET.

0722 GMT - Amundi's third-party distribution client segment is a key growth pillar, Citi says in a research note. The French asset manager updated its outlook for the segment, which is taking share in a growing market. It has achieved a 21% compound annual growth rate for its assets under management over the 2020-24 period, analysts write. The business has around 600 distribution contracts and is already well-diversified by geography and client type. The demand from distributors for players that offer breadth and efficient scaling benefits incumbents like Amundi. "We came away reassured by the strength and breadth of Amundi's offering, its strong market positioning, as well as its long-term growth trajectory, supported by attractive M&A optionality," Citi says. (elena.vardon@wsj.com)

0710 GMT - There aren't any significant sector-specific risks that would justify a strong correction for Spanish banks, Renta 4 says in a research note. Though, markets at record highs call for caution, it says. "We believe corrections, if any, would be more responsive to the market environment in a context of high uncertainty (trade war, impact of tariffs, armed conflicts, escalating geopolitical tensions)," analyst Nuria Alvarez writes. The domestic profile of the lenders provides them with support in this climate, she says. The sector's earnings have proven resilient despite interest-rate cuts, supporting capital distributions and a share rally since the start of the year. The next catalyst is the publication of second-quarter results, in which some could lift their guidance. Renta 4 lowers its rating on Santander and UniCaja to hold from overweight. (elena.vardon@wsj.com)

0624 GMT - Societe Generale's digital bank BoursoBank is a key element of the French lender's investment case, Jefferies says in a research note. Analyst Joseph Dickerson and associate Theo Massing estimate that BoursoBank will earn the group 325 million euros in 2026, providing a mechanical 5% uplift to overall profits. They note that the digital bank is still investing heavily in customer acquisition though these costs are set to fall in the coming years. It is remarkable that its number of employees has plateaued at around 1,000 while client numbers keep growing, they note. The analysts see BoursoBank capable of achieving a return on equity of 25% by 2026, which supports the profitability improvement of SocGen's French retail unit. (elena.vardon@wsj.com)

0442 GMT - ANZ's efforts to transform its Australian retail bank are seen as a key share price driver by Morgan Stanley analysts, albeit one that is hard to quantify. The MS analysts point out that the potential benefits are long-dated and that the timeline remains uncertain. They tell clients in a note that ANZ's new CEO could accelerate the roll-out of the lender's new digital platform. Migrating customers onto the platform could have negative implications for margins or customer churn, they add. MS has an equal-weight rating and a A$26.50 target price on the stock, which is down 1.6% at A$28.66. (stuart.condie@wsj.com)

0258 GMT - Thailand banks' asset quality and profitability prospects this year could be hindered by near-term challenges, Fitch Ratings analysts write in commentary. The country's growth seems likely to continue underperforming regional peers over the next few years, constraining business opportunities for banks, they say. The Bank of Thailand started its rate cut cycle in 4Q 2024, which will lead to thinner margins for banks. Rate cuts are expected to be limited compared with the aggressive monetary easing during the pandemic, Fitch says. It expects narrower margins and weak growth prospects to have a modest effect on banks' 2025-2026 profitability. Fitch revises its 2025 outlook for the Thai banking sector to deteriorating from neutral. (amanda.lee@wsj.com)

0112 GMT - Hong Leong Bank's risk-reward profile likely improved, driven by a rally in its China associate Bank of Chengdu and rising prospects for dividends, CIMB Securities analyst Rachel Huang says in a note. Bank of Chengdu's strong share price performance and improving consensus forecasts suggest further upside, with a potential Hong Leong Bank's valuation of MYR24.10 if earnings assumptions are revised upward, she says. Also, Basel III rule changes could boost Hong Leong Bank's Common Equity Tier 1 ratio, allowing it to raise its dividend payout ratio and deliver higher dividend yields, she adds. CIMB upgrades Hong Leong Bank's rating to buy from hold, and its raises target price to MYR21.50 from MYR21.40. Shares are unchanged at MYR19.40. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

0041 GMT - Can Centuria Capital deliver significant growth in assets under management without raising more equity? UBS thinks it's unlikely. Still, the bank notes the environment for raising equity is improving. Centuria Capital has managed to grow pockets of assets under management, such as in agriculture, shopping centers and private credit. But the pace of growth has slowed relative to history. "As asset values stabilise and interest rates reduce over time, Centuria Capital should be in a better position to raise equity for new funds," UBS says. The bank expects progress to be modest, but it should reflect an improvement after recent challenging years. (david.winning@wsj.com; @dwinningWSJ)

1152 GMT - Deutsche Pfandbriefbank's portfolio winddown will likely lead to major extraordinary costs which could result in losses this year, Warburg Research says in a note. The German lender said it will withdraw from its commercial property financing business in the U.S. There should be good demand from potential buyers for these assets, which make up a portfolio with a total volume of 4.1 billion euros, analyst Andreas Plaesier writes. The group's indication that it expects to maintain its CET1 capital ratio of 14% at all times is helpful to determine the expected maximum loss in the event of a reduction, which he calculates at around 200 million euros. The reduction should release equity which can be used to invest in its new European business, expand the commission business or enhance capital distributions. Shares fall 2.6%, extending yesterday's losses. (elena.vardon@wsj.com)

0839 GMT - Switzerland's proposed new banking rules, which include tougher capital requirements for UBS Group, could be watered down, analysts at RBC Capital Markets say in a research note. The Swiss government assumes that UBS can mitigate the impact of the changes, the analysts say. However, it isn't clear how asset sales or partial IPOs among other measures could mitigate this without adding debt elsewhere or hurting UBS's long-term capital generation, according to RBC. "Given our work suggests less scope for mitigation than the government assumes, we expect the proposal to be watered down, eventually," the analysts say. RBC cuts its target price on UBS to 29 Swiss francs from 31.50 francs. Shares fall 1.8% to 24.75 francs. (adria.calatayud@wsj.com)

data.title: Energy & Utilities Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Energy and Utilities. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires at 4:20 ET, 12:20 ET and 16:50 ET.

0815 GMT - Doosan Fuel Cell is set to get a revenue boost from an expected increase in U.S.-bound shipments, NH Investment & Securities analysts write in a note. The South Korean fuel-cell maker will likely manufacture fuel cells for its U.S. subsidiary, HyAxiom, starting in 2H, they say. Analysts Y.S. Jung and J.H. Gwak raise their revenue forecast for the company by 8% for 2025 and 11% for 2026. They expect Doosan to win more contracts in the U.S., given the solid demand for power-generation facilities in data centers there. The U.S. fuel-cell market grows 15% to 20% annually, they note. (kwanwoo.jun@wsj.com)

0757 GMT - Cracks in Asia's economic growth could appear if oil prices rise to around $85/bbl, but the impact is likely to remain manageable, HSBC chief Asia economist Frederic Neumann writes in a note. Oil wouldget more attention from central bankers at that level, as it would contribute to inflation rather than detract from it, Neumann says. Still, the oil and gas intensity of Asian economies has fallen sharply over the past decade, and in real terms, oil is quite cheap at the moment, he adds. If oil rises much higher than $85/bbl, it would deliver a major hit, he says. Front-month WTI crude oil futures are 0.7% higher at $75.66/bbl; front-month Brent crude oil futures are 2.4% lower at $76.94/bbl. (kimberley.kao@wsj.com)

0031 GMT - Regulatory risk involved in the XRG-led consortium's bid for Santos is not only elevated but difficult to isolate or mitigate, says Morgans. The consortium is scrutinizing Santos's books to determine whether it will make a cash offer of A$8.89/share, equivalent to US$5.76, binding. Analyst Adrian Prendergast points out that Santos owns and operates assets designated as "critical infrastructure" by law, placing it squarely in the national security category. "This classification makes the transaction far more sensitive," especially given XRG's parent--Abu Dhabi National Oil Co.--is owned by a foreign government, Morgans says. Even a restructure or asset split is unlikely to resolve this issue, citing Santos's equity in the GLNG gas-export project, Morgans says. (david.winning@wsj.com; @dwinningWSJ)

data.title: China's Midyear Shopping Festival Pulls In Record Online Sales
data.description:
By Tracy Qu

China's second-largest shopping event of the year generated record sales, with online spending by consumers exceeding $100 billion as government measures bolstered consumption among cautious households.

The value of goods sold by Chinese e-commerce platforms reached an all-time high during the "618" shopping festival that took place from mid-May to mid-June this year, according to figures from data provider Syntun.

Gross merchandise value across shopping sites soared 15% to 855.6 billion yuan, equivalent to $119.03 billion, Syntun said. This year's tally included an extra week of promotions.

Once a one-day campaign to mark online-shopping giant JD.com's June 18 founding anniversary, the event now spans weeks, coming second only to November's Singles' Day extravaganza started by rival Alibaba Group.

The strong sales figures suggest that Chinese policymakers' efforts to get wary households to spend again are paying off.

Economic woes--including dimming job prospects and a yearslong property-sector slump that has eroded household wealth--have left consumers reluctant to open their wallets.

To help boost consumption, Beijing has implemented a series of measures, including subsidies for goods such as smartphones and household appliances. Customers placing orders on e-commerce platforms are also eligible for subsidies of around 15% on certain products.

JD.com said the number of users who placed orders on its platform during this year's 618 event more than doubled from 2024. Its platform also drew a record number of daily active users during the period.

Alibaba's Tmall claimed the No. 1 spot in Syntun's ranking of 618 sales by gross merchandise value, beating out JD.com. In third and fourth place were ByteDance's Douyin and PDD's Pinduoduo.

None of those companies disclosed total sales figures for the event, although Alibaba said that 453 brands on Taobao and Tmall surpassed 100 million yuan in GMV during the sales period. Brands that surpassed 1 billion yuan in GMV included Apple, Xiaomi, Huawei, Nike, Adidas, L'Oreal and Lululemon, it said.

While subsidies have so far boosted sales of certain goods, analysts have questioned what will happen once the initial impulse fades. Some warn that the measures are drawing forward purchases, and there will be a payback effect later on.

"Consumers also seem to be more immune to promotional discounts," Citi analysts led by Alicia Yap wrote in a note. "With more rational and selective spending behavior, consumers (esp. the younger generation) are likely only to spend when they need to."

Write to Tracy Qu at tracy.qu@wsj.com

data.title: Auto & Transport Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering the Auto and Transport sector. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires at 4:20 ET, 12:20 ET and 16:50 ET.

0755 GMT - A pause in trade-in subsidies will likely slow auto sales in the near term, Daiwa analysts write in a note. At least six Chinese cities and municipalities have suspended trade-in subsidies for car buyers in June, they add, citing a media report. While Shenyang and Chongqing said the suspension was due to adjustments to improve capital efficiency, most other cities attributed the pause to the depletion of the first round of nationwide funding totaling 162 billion yuan, they say. Car sales should accelerate after the second round of subsidies totaling 138 billion yuan is effectively allocated by the central government later this year, they say. Daiwa expects the auto sector to remain under pressure until July, with sentiment likely to recover toward the end of the third quarter.(jiahui.huang@wsj.com; @ivy_jiahuihuang)

1247 GMT - Volvo's key end markets of trucks and construction equipment are on the cusp of recovery and its peer-leading margins should expand further, Bank of America Securities analysts write. The bank forecasts a second-quarter industrial adjusted EBIT of 12.6 billion Swedish kronor, down 31% on year. North American truck deliveries are expected to be weak and margins face headwinds from tariff costs and krona appreciation. Solid orders in Europe should offset weakness in North America though. The bank says near-term profit expectations are now sufficiently rebased, while re-rating potential will outweigh any further cuts to earnings expectations. "The current share price therefore offers a particularly attractive entry point." BofA Securities lifts its american depositary receipt price target to $36 from $35. Volvo's ADRs closed at $26.72. (dominic.chopping@wsj.com)

0836 GMT - Xiaomi's SU7 sedan was a success thanks to its product competitiveness, marketing strategy, brand power and ecosystem integration, Bernstein analysts write in a note. The SU7 features a stylish and attractive exterior, with a spacious and plush interior equipped with details and gadgets, they say. The company's marketing savviness also boosted momentum, they add. The company capitalized on its smartphone sales experience to create impactful marketing campaigns, effectively using social media to generate buzz and engage potential customers, they say. Leveraging the power of Xiaomi's brand in smartphone and Internet-of-Things, the company's extensive product portfolio enhances brand reach that dwarfs its EV peers, they say. (jiahui.huang@wsj.com; @ivy_jiahuihuang)

data.title: Viva Goods Shares Surge Following Li Ning Stake Boost
data.description:
Viva Goods shares closed 19% higher on Friday after the company boosted its indirect equity interests in associate Li Ning .

Viva China Development purchased 18,092,500 shares in sports brand Li Ning for nearly HK$275 million, according to a late Thursday filing with the Hong Kong bourse.

The shares, acquired from the open market between Jan. 10 and June 19, represent around 0.7% of Li Ning's issued share capital.

As a result, Viva China Development now holds 290,213,043 Li Ning shares, equivalent to an 11.23% stake.

data.title: Cricut Insider Sold Shares Worth $1,178,436, According to a Recent SEC Filing
data.description:
Arora Ashish, 10% Owner, Director, Chief Executive Officer, on June 16, 2025, sold 180,000 shares in Cricut for $1,178,436. Following the Form 4 filing with the SEC, Ashish has control over a total of 2,931,931 Class A common shares of the company, with 2,931,931 shares held directly.

SEC Filing:

www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1828962/000182896225000109/xslF345X05/wk-form4_1750281466.xml

data.title: Karelian Diamond Resources Plans Equity Raise to Advance Projects in Finland, Northern Ireland
data.description:
Karelian Diamond Resources raised 185,000 pounds sterling through a placement and subscription of new shares.

The diamond exploration company placed 18 million shares and issued for subscription another 6,666,666 shares, both at 0.0075 pound apiece, according to a Friday filing. Trading of the new shares on the London bourse's AIM segment is anticipated to take place on June 26.

The proceeds will be used to launch the next phase of its diamond deposit development work at the Lahtojoki diamond deposit in Finland, to assess the impact of copper mineralization at the Cappagh copper mine site in Northern Ireland, and as additional working capital.

data.title: Situation at Iran's Bushehr nuclear plant is 'normal', Russian official says
data.description:
Russian nuclear energy chief Alexei Likhachev said on Friday that the situation at Iran's Bushehr nuclear power plant, where hundreds of Russian specialists work, was "normal" and under control.

The Israeli military said at one point on Thursday that it had struck the Russian-built Bushehr facility, but later said the comment had been made by mistake. Likhachev, head of the state nuclear energy corporation Rosatom, said on Thursday that any attack on the plant could cause a Chernobyl-style nuclear disaster.

Bushehr is Iran's only operating nuclear power plant and uses Russian fuel that Russia then takes back when it is spent to reduce proliferation risk.

Asked on Friday about the situation at Bushehr, Likhachev told reporters: "So far the situation is completely under control, everything is normal. The night passed in the usual anxious but manageable atmosphere."

He added: "We continue to work in pre-mobilization mode and very much hope that all our signals from yesterday reached the Israeli leadership."

The "pre-mobilisation" comment appeared to refer to the possibility of evacuating staff in the event of an emergency.

Russian President Vladimir Putin said this week that Russia has up to 600 staff at Bushehr, including 250 permanent workers and others on temporary assignment. He said Israel had issued a promise to Russia over their safety.

Likhachev said on Thursday that some of those on assignment had been evacuated but the core staff remained for now.

Russia, which has close ties with Iran, has warned strongly against U.S. military intervention on the side of Israel in the air war that began a week ago.

Israel has stated that it launched strikes on Iran to prevent it from obtaining a nuclear weapon. Iran, which denies such an intention, has responded with drone and missile attacks on Israeli cities.

data.title: Greece's current account deficit shrinks in April
data.description:
Greece's current account deficit shrank to 2.1 billion euros in April from 2.5 billion euros in the same month last year on the back of higher tourism revenues and falling imports, Bank of Greece showed on Friday.

Revenues from tourism, a major economic driver for the Mediterranean country, rose by an annual 17.4% to 1.1 billion as foreign arrivals increased by 6.4%, the central bank said. Imports dropped by 11.5%.

data.title: China’s Hello Kitty gifts investors a mystery box
data.description:
Labubu, the toothy-grinned plush doll, has powered a 500% rally at Hong Kong-listed Pop Mart, now worth $41 bln. Investors may be betting on the firm's push into theme parks and more. But as the iconic Japanese character shows, turning a fad into lasting value is no smooth ride.

Full view will be published shortly.

Follow Karen Kwok on LinkedIn and X.

CONTEXT NEWS

"Blind box" toymaker Pop Mart International, which has seen frenzied sales worldwide for products related to its ugly-cute Labubu character, opened its first jewellery store in Shanghai on June 13.

A Beijing auction house sold a human-sized Labubu figure for 1.08 million yuan ($150,275.51) on June 10, setting a new record for the "blind box" toy as it moves from craze to collectable.

People’s Daily, the Chinese Communist Party’s official newspaper, on June 20 criticised the “blind box” phenomenon, advocating for stricter regulation. The article did not mention Pop Mart by name and focused more on children and young people who were spending heavily on unmarked packets to collect cards. Share prices of Pop Mart fell 4% on June 20.

Share prices of Pop Mart jumped 496% in the past year, trading at HK$238.6 on June 20.

data.title: Italy's Annual Construction Output Rises 5.9% in April
data.description:
Italy's calendar-adjusted construction output index climbed 5.9% year over year in April, following a revised 3.8% increase in March, statistics agency Istat said Friday.

On a monthly basis, Italy's seasonally adjusted construction output rose 2.4%, against the 0.6% decline earlier.

data.title: Dow Jones Top Energy Headlines at 4 AM ET: Cameco Stock and Other Ways to Play the Overseas Nuclear Energy Boom | Chevron ...
data.description:
Cameco Stock and Other Ways to Play the Overseas Nuclear Energy Boom

There are currently no new reactors under construction in the U.S., but American companies have plenty of international business.

----

Chevron and Exxon Are Really AI Plays. We Explain.

AI needs power-and lots of it. These formerly stodgy Big Oil companies are well positioned to offer just that.

----

Oil Prices Are Jumping. What History Says Happens When Conflicts Threaten Supply.

For the past 40 years, the biggest increases in oil prices have all been driven by demand, not supply.

----

Clean Energy Projects Hinge on Senate Megabill Showdown

The tug of war over renewable subsidies and jobs has centered on senators like North Carolina's Thom Tillis.

----

History shows what has to happen for the Israel-Iran conflict to trigger an oil spike

Deposing Iran's Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei and his regime may cause a sustained spike in oil prices

----

Nvidia Goes Nuclear, Investing in Bill Gates' Start-Up

NVentures, Nvidia's venture-capital arm, was part of the latest funding round for TerraPower, a nuclear developer founded by Bill Gates.

----

How a Solar Factory in the Heartland Sees the 'Big Beautiful Bill'

The general manager of the Bila Solar unit in Indianapolis, said legislation could impede plans to expand the company's solar factory.

----

Oil Edges Up as Next Steps in Iran Conflict Eyed

Investors remained tentative as they wait for the next development in the conflict between Iran and Israel. Front-month crude oil finish 0.4% higher.

----

U.S. Oil Stocks Post 11.5 Million Barrel Drop

Meanwhile, gasoline inventories rose by 200,000 barrels, off from analyst forecasts of a 400,000-barrel increase.

----

Keyera Deal for Certain Plains NGL Assets Widens Reach, CEO Says

Keyera's deal for Plains All American Pipeline's natural gas liquids business in Canada will bolster the energy-infrastructure company's scale and widen its reach into Eastern Canada, while bringing flexibility in market access and driving growth, says Keyera CEO Dean Setoguchi.

----

AI 'Factories' Are Hungry for Power. Expect 'Gigawatt-Scale' Growth.

The increase in demand isn't expected to slow soon.

----

This oil gauge signals how nervous investors are about Trump's warning to Iran

U.S. and global benchmark oil prices have seen their share of big moves since Israel launched attacks on Iran late last week. But there's one indicator that helps put into perspective just how worried investors have become about the potential scope of the conflict.

----

Energy Stocks Have Had a Mixed Start to 2025. Natural Gas Is Hot and Oil Is Not.

The energy sector is holding its own with the S&P 500, thanks to surging natural gas stocks. Oil stocks may start to catch up though.

----

Energy & Utilities Roundup: Market Talk

Find insight on Secure Waste Infrastructure, Tullow Oil and more in the latest Market Talks covering energy and utilities..

data.title: Doosan Fuel Cell Set to Get Boost From U.S.-Bound Shipments — Market Talk
data.description: Doosan Fuel Cell is set to get a revenue boost from an expected increase in U.S.-bound shipments, NH Investment & Securities analysts write in a note. The South Korean fuel-cell maker will likely manufacture fuel cells for its U.S. subsidiary, HyAxiom, starting in 2H, they say. Analysts Y.S. Jung and J.H. Gwak raise their revenue forecast for the company by 8% for 2025 and 11% for 2026. They expect Doosan to win more contracts in the U.S., given the solid demand for power-generation facilities in data centers there. The U.S. fuel-cell market grows 15% to 20% annually, they note. (kwanwoo.jun@wsj.com)

data.title: PRESSR: The Department of Health – Abu Dhabi and Abbot unite to manufacture pharmaceuticals locally in Abu Dhabi
data.description:
ABU DHABI, UAE /PRNewswire/ — During BIO International Convention in Boston USA, the Department of Health – Abu Dhabi (DoH), the regulator of the healthcare sector in Abu Dhabi, and Abbott, the global healthcare leader, entered a landmark partnership to localise manufacturing of pharmaceuticals in Abu Dhabi and advance digital health solutions. Leveraging Abu Dhabi's advanced life sciences infrastructure, this milestone reflects the emirate's proactive preparedness to reduce dependency on imports, strengthen supply chain resilience, and foster a sustainable, self-sufficient healthcare ecosystem.

H.E. Dr. Noura Al Ghaithi, Undersecretary of the Department of Health – Abu Dhabi, said: "Following the recent launch of the Health, Endurance, Longevity and Medicine (HELM) Cluster in Abu Dhabi, the emirate proved to be equipped with a holistic, integrated infrastructure that fosters local manufacturing and R&D, positioning it as a regional healthcare gateway and leader in life sciences. By integrating our strategic vision with Abbott's expertise, we aim to accelerate the development and deployment of innovative healthcare products and solutions, reducing time-to-market, improving healthcare outcomes, and ensuring that breakthrough therapies reach patients faster. This approach positions Abu Dhabi as a regional leader in fostering an agile, innovation-driven healthcare ecosystem that directly translates scientific advancements into tangible benefits for our community."

The strategic collaboration outlines a comprehensive plan focusing on four key pillars including localisation of Abbott's pharmaceutical portfolio, biosimilar development, digital transformation through electronic patient information leaflets, and a robust education and workforce development initiative.

Mazen Bachir, Regional Director for Abbott's established pharmaceuticals business in the Gulf, Emerging Markets and Levant said: "Abbott is proud to announce a strategic collaboration with the Department of Health – Abu Dhabi, aimed at advancing healthcare resilience and innovation in Abu Dhabi. Building on Abbott's long-standing commitment to the UAE's healthcare system, this partnership will focus on localising existing pharmaceutical products, and jointly exploring the development of biosimilars, supported by regulatory alignment. The collaboration also includes initiatives to digitise life science product information through electronic leaflets in alignment with the UAE's digital health strategy. Another key component of this collaboration is the development of education programs to build local capabilities. Additionally, Abbott and the Department of Health will evaluate opportunities for joint research and development to strengthen long-term healthcare sustainability in the UAE."

Led by DoH, a high-level delegation has embarked on a strategic mission to the United States from June 15 to 21, 2025. The delegation will conduct over 20 strategic meetings and visits with public and private sector leaders across the U.S., aimed at knowledge exchange, investment opportunities and the signing of new agreements that accelerate the adoption of advanced health solutions.

Representing Abu Dhabi's innovation ecosystem, the delegation includes key stakeholders such as the Abu Dhabi Investment Office, Mubadala BIO, M42, Masdar City, KEZAD, PureHealth, and Etihad Cargo, New York University Abu Dhabi (NYUAD), Khalifa University, startAD and Mohamed bin Zayed University of Artificial Intelligence (MBZUAI).

data.title: Snowflake Insider Sold Shares Worth $1,517,152, According to a Recent SEC Filing
data.description:
Frank Slootman, Director, on June 16, 2025, sold 7,294 shares in Snowflake for $1,517,152. Following the Form 4 filing with the SEC, Slootman has control over a total of 1,038,497 Class A common shares of the company, with 170,718 shares held directly and 867,779 controlled indirectly.

SEC Filing:

www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1640147/000164014725000127/xslF345X05/wk-form4_1750287562.xml

data.title: Kenyan shilling flat Vs dollar - LSEG data
data.description:
The Kenyan shilling was flat against the dollar on Friday from the previous session, data from the London Stock Exchange Group (LSEG) showed.

At 0717 GMT commercial banks quoted the shilling at 129.00/129.50, same level as Thursday's close.

data.title: Greece stocks take another step closer to potential rating upgrade
data.description:
GREECE STOCKS TAKE ANOTHER STEP CLOSER TO POTENTIAL RATING UPGRADE

MSCI came out with its annual market accessibility review on global equity markets and things are looking pretty bright for Greece.

The index provider confirmed that measures taken by the government to improve its economic fundamentals along with market reforms were bearing fruit, putting its equities market on track to achieve developed-market status.

MSCI said measures introduced by authorities to manage pooled assets and stimulate stock lending and short-selling activity were adopted and validated by market participants - key issues that the index provider had flagged, not just in its 2024 review, but also when it downgraded the market to 'emerging market' status in 2013.

In March, Moody's became the last among major credit rating agencies to lift Greece's debt rating to "investment grade", acknowledging the efforts taken to improve debt sustainability.

Athens equities (.ATG) have jumped over 100% since 2021 and has outperformed both Europe's STOXX 600 and the broader MSCI emerging markets stocks index CBOE:EFS so far this year.

Greece has made a steady recovery from a debt crisis that started in 2009 and almost saw it drop out of the euro before an international bailout. However, as a sign of its comeback, the government has been repaying bailout loans and debt ahead of schedule.

Its banks - which had to be bailed out during the crisis - are fully privatised again and some of them have also started paying dividends.

Other index providers including S&P Dow Jones and FTSE Russell have put the Athens stock market on a watchlist for a potential upgrade to developed market status.

However, the market classification report by MSCI due on June 24 is the one that major fund managers will be keeping a keen eye on.

(Johann M Cherian)

*****

data.title: Kroger, Accenture Earnings and Leading Indicators: What to Watch Friday — WSJ
data.description:
By WSJ Staff

U.S. stock and bond markets reopen Friday after the Juneteenth holiday. Here is what else to watch:

  • Earnings: Supermarket chain Kroger, consultancy firm Accenture, CarMax and Darden Restaurants before the bell.
  • Economic data: Philadelphia Federal Reserve manufacturing survey at 8:30 a.m. ET, followed by leading economic indicators.
  • Overseas: Meeting of the European Union's Economic and Financial Affairs Council.

This item is part of a Wall Street Journal live coverage event. The full stream can be found by searching P/WSJL (WSJ Live Coverage).
